company_type: Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) - Multifamily Residential

supply_chain.dependencies: Development and construction activities rely on third-party contractors, suppliers, and materials. The Company may use third-party developers and general contractors in expansion regions where it has limited resources or scale.
supply_chain.risks: Inflation or tariffs may increase costs of materials, labor and services from third-party contractors and suppliers. Supply chain disruptions could impact construction costs and schedules. Changes in immigration laws or their enforcement could impact labor availability and costs.

regulatory_risks: Rent control and rent stabilization laws in California (statewide rent control limiting increases to lesser of 10% or 5% plus local CPI for communities older than 15 years)
Rent control and rent stabilization laws in New York (limits on rent increases for rent-controlled and rent-stabilized units)
Emergency orders in California limiting rent increases to 10% during declared states of emergency (e.g., wildfires)
State and local regulations limiting ability to charge market rents, increase rents, use algorithmic pricing tools, charge non-rent fees, or evict tenants

Antitrust litigation and investigations related to use of revenue management systems (District of Columbia Antitrust Litigation, Maryland Antitrust Litigation, U.S. Department of Justice lawsuit against other multifamily providers)
Proposed federal and state legislation to regulate use of algorithmic revenue management systems (San Francisco has adopted such legislation)
Zoning laws, building codes, and requirements for accessibility under Americans with Disabilities Act and Fair Housing Act
Environmental laws and regulations governing wetlands protection, storm water runoff, wastewater discharge, asbestos, lead paint, mold, and hazardous substances
REIT qualification requirements under Internal Revenue Code Section 422
Federal securities laws and regulations, NYSE listing requirements
Privacy and data security laws (California Consumer Privacy Act as amended by California Privacy Rights Act, Texas Data Privacy and Security Act)
Climate-related disclosure requirements (SEC rules, California legislation)
Tax protection payment obligations from Archstone Acquisition (estimated $43,815,000 if triggering actions taken in 2024)
